label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Jennifer could have gone to the swimming pool. The problem gives me a list of events that different people saw Jennifer doing at certain times, and I need to find out the possible time window when she wasn't doing those things but could have gone swimming. Also, the swimming pool is closed after 10pm, so whatever time she went must be before that. First, I'll list out all the times Jennifer was seen doing other activities based on the information provided. 1. Jennifer woke up at 5am. That's the start of her day, but we don't know how long she was up. But the next event is Richard seeing her at the office from 5am to 8am. So she was at the office during that time. 2. Then, Samantha saw Jennifer reading at the library from 12pm to 1pm. So between noon and 1pm, she was at the library. 3. Betty saw her buying a bike at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. So from 1 to 2pm, she was at the bike shop. 4. Sarah saw Jennifer buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 9pm. That's a long time—she was there from 2pm until 9pm. 5. Thomas saw her walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. So between 9 and 10pm, she was heading there. Also, the swimming pool was closed after 10pm, so she must have gone before that. Now, the question is: between what times could Jennifer have gone to the swimming pool? The options are (A) 8am to 12pm, (B) 9pm to 10pm, (C) 2pm to 9pm, and (D) 12pm to 1pm. Let me check each time interval to see when Jennifer wasn't occupied with other activities. Starting from
